victim.value: A cockroach narrator killed by a human with a sandal
victim.quote: As I lie there helpless on the tiles, only my antennae moving slowly, I cry for my babies

culprit_and_motive.value: Human kills cockroach out of disgust/revulsion

resolution.value: Cockroach is killed but declares species will survive through reproduction
resolution.quote: My babies will have babies and they'll have others and no matter how hard you try to erase us

investigator.value: The cockroach victim serves as first-person narrator
investigator.quote: I stand frozen, hiding in the shadows, aware of my misstep.

red_herring.value: The narrative misdirects readers to see giant mythical creatures when the 'gods' are revealed to be ordinary humans in a shower
red_herring.quote: I swear these creatures are gods. They possess gargantuan size, incredible strength, and somehow they make light appear

clue_fairness.value: All clues about the true scale are present: tiles, antennae, the reveal of cockroach perspective available throughout
clue_fairness.quote: As I lie there helpless on the tiles, only my antennae moving slowly

social_world.value: Hierarchical species warfare with humans as dominant oppressors and cockroaches as survival-focused underclass
social_world.quote: Our deep hatred for each others species began with their total disregard with our right to exist

reveal_mechanic.value: Perspective shift revelation through physical details: tiles, antennae, and human speech expose the cockroach narrator

investigator_wound.value: Parental desperation driving the narrator to risk death searching for missing mate and food for offspring
investigator_wound.quote: Putting my children's safety before my own, I began my journey only to find my life hanging in the balance

_meta.title: ebook nomar knight they can 039 t kill us all
